Dear Ms. Campbell:

L.ONG BEACH, CAL! FOR f'..; :A 90801 
(2!3) 435-6565 
(2 
775-3206 
It was a pleasure to speak to you today concerning campaign 
disclosure requirements for the No On IV Committee for the 
forthcoming March 20, 1979 Special Municipal Election to be 
held in Long Beach, California. 
You provided me with much 
useful information and alleviated most of my anxiety with 
regard to late contribution filings pursuant to California 
Government Code §84214 and the post-election disclosure filing 
required of the Committee under §84200 of the same code. 
The purpose of this letter is to confirm the conclusions we 
reached today. 
I informed you that the No On IV Committee 
today received a $35,000 contribution from North American 
Philips Lighting Corporation of Hightstown, New Jersey. 
The 
same amount was immediately deposited in the Committee's 
account in the Farmers & Merchants Bank at 302 Pine Avenue, 
Long Beach. 
You, in turn, informed me that the occasion of 
this contribution required a 
late contribution" filing by 
both the Recipient Committee and North American Philips 
Lighting Corporation within 48 hours. 
Since the appropriate 
filing location for proponents and opponents of local ballot 
measures is the office of the City Clerk and that office is 
closed on Sunday, March 18, 1979, when the 48 hour time period 
expires, you informed me that a Monday filing, r1arch 19, 1979, 
would satisfy the requirements of Government Code §84214. 
Later, in the same conversation, we discussed post-election 
filing requirements for both the No On IV Cornmittee and North 
American Philips Lighting Corporation. 
First you told me that 
the Committee's next and final filing obligation would be 65 
days subsequent to the March 20, 1979 election and that the 
appropriate form for filing was Form 420. 
You then informed 


Barbara Campbell 
March 16, 1979 
Page Two 
me that since North American Philips Lighting Corporation con-
tributed more than $5,000 within a calendar year to political 
endeavors, it was required to file a Major Donor form, Form 461, 
including all contributions and independent expenditures made 
for political purposes. 
Both the No On IV Committee and North American Philips Lighting 
Corporation are relying on the above advice. 
Should any of the 
statements contained in the preceding paragraph not coincide with 
your recollection of our conversation, please inform me at the 
earliest possible moment either by mail or by telephone. 
Prior to our conversation, I spoke with Gene Morrow in the 
Secretary of State's Office and he provided me with the same 
information on which you and I have agreed. 
Once again, I want 
to thank you for your assistance. 
Yours very truly, 
KILPATRICK, CLAYTON, MEYER & MADDEN 
~W.0ti64 
Douglas W. Otto 
DWOsjc

sections.question_synthetic: What are the late contribution reporting requirements and post-election filing obligations for the No On IV Committee and North American Philips Lighting Corporation regarding a $35,000 contribution made before a March 20, 1979 special election in Long Beach?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC clarifies that North American Phillips Lighting Corporation was required to file a late contribution report within 48 hours of making the $35,000 contribution, and the No On IV Committee was similarly required to file its late contribution report within 48 hours. Additionally, the FPPC modifies previous advice regarding post-election filing obligations and provides guidance on committee termination under Government Code Section 18404.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both incoming request letter (March 16, 1979 from Douglas W. Otto) and FPPC response letter (March 28, 1979 from Barbara Campbell). No formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections with headers; information extracted from letter body. Some OCR degradation present in source document affecting readability. Focus of extraction is the FPPC response portion.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the late contribution reporting requirements and post-election filing obligations for the No On IV Committee and North American Philips Lighting Corporation regarding a $35,000 contribution made before a March 20, 1979 special election in Long Beach?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC clarifies that North American Phillips Lighting Corporation was required to file a late contribution report within 48 hours of making the $35,000 contribution, and the No On IV Committee was similarly required to file its late contribution report within 48 hours. Additionally, the FPPC modifies previous advice regarding post-election filing obligations and provides guidance on committee termination under Government Code Section 18404.

embedding.summary: FPPC response letter from Barbara Campbell (Counsel, Legal Division) to Philip M. Madden dated March 28, 1979, providing two modifications to previous advice given regarding late contribution reporting requirements under Government Code Section 84214 and post-election filing obligations for the No On IV Committee's $35,000 contribution from North American Phillips Lighting Corporation.
